name: Carrizo de la Ribera passing through Fresno del Camino — circular by Astorga
name_original: Carrizo de la Ribera pasando por Fresno del Camino — circular por Astorga
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Valverde De La Virgen, located in the province of León, Spain, traverse open, rolling landscapes. The region features plateau scenery with flat or gently undulating stretches, making it suitable for easy touring bike trails. These cycling routes Valverde De La Virgen are integrated into significant historical paths like the Jacobean Route and the Via de la Plata, offering long distance touring bike routes with cultural landmarks. Best touring cycling routes around Valverde De La Virgen The most popular touring cycling route is Carrizo de la Ribera passing through Fresno del Camino — circular by Astorga, a 27.9 miles (44.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 24 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic riding through the countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Casa Botines Gaudí Museum – Plaza Mayor loop from Quintana-Raneros, a moderate 42.8 miles (68.8 km) path. This route provides opportunities to explore historical cities and cultural landmarks. Local touring cyclists also love the Oak Forest Trail – Cerro Monteo loop from Quintana-Raneros, a 59.3 miles (95.4 km) trail leading through open countryside, often completed in about 8 hours 35 minutes. Touring cycling around Valverde De La Virgen is defined by open, rolling landscapes, plateau scenery, and historical routes like the Jacobean Route and Via de la Plata. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy to difficult. The routes in Valverde De La Virgen are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.7 stars from more than 140 reviews. More than 2500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Valverde De La Virgen's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Valverde De La Virgen?
A: There are over 140 touring cycling routes around Valverde De La Virgen. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 24 easy routes, 77 moderate routes, and 43 difficult routes available for exploration.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Valverde De La Virgen?
A: The region around Valverde De La Virgen is characterized by open, rolling landscapes and plateau scenery. You can expect mostly flat or gently undulating stretches, making it ideal for leisurely touring rides without significant technical difficulty.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Valverde De La Virgen offers 24 easy touring cycling routes. A great option for a relaxed ride is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40319422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Graffiti Photo Spot – Sunrise loop from Trobajo del Camino&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10.4 miles (16.7 km) long and features minimal elevation gain, perfect for a comfortable outing.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Valverde De La Virgen are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363117203/carrizo-de-la-ribera-passing-through-fresno-del-camino-circular-by-astorga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrizo de la Ribera passing through Fresno del Camino — circular by Astorga&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate loop covering 27.9 miles (44.9 km) through scenic countryside.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Valverde De La Virgen is rich in history, being part of the Jacobean Route (Camino de Santiago) and the Via de la Plata. You can explore significant historical cities and cultural landmarks. Notable attractions nearby include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/395182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;León Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5658871" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman City Walls of León&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4590991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Botines Gaudí Museum&lt;/a&gt;. The church tower of Valverde de la Virgen and the parish church of Santa Engracia also serve as quaint points of interest.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Valverde De La Virgen?
A: The touring cycling routes in Valverde De La Virgen are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.7 stars from over 140 reviews. Cyclists often praise the open, rolling landscapes, the solitude of the wide horizons, and the unique blend of cultural immersion offered by routes integrated into historical paths like the Jacobean Route.

Q: Are there any longer touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer challenge, there are several routes, including 43 difficult options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38188945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oak Forest Trail – Cerro Monteo loop from Quintana-Raneros&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 59.3 miles (95.4 km) trail that leads through open countryside, typically taking over 8 hours to complete.

Q: Is it possible to explore historical cities and towns by bike from Valverde De La Virgen?
A: Yes, the region's connection to the Via de la Plata means you can cycle through or near significant historical cities. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40014153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Botines Gaudí Museum – Plaza Mayor loop from Quintana-Raneros&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 42.8 miles (68.8 km) route that provides excellent opportunities to explore historical cities and cultural landmarks, including the famous Gaudí Museum.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Valverde De La Virgen?
A: The open, rolling landscapes of Valverde De La Virgen are generally pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, but the flat terrain makes it manageable. Winter offers a different, often quieter, experience, though you should check local weather conditions.

Q: Are there opportunities for solitude and experiencing open countryside on these routes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes around Valverde De La Virgen offer tranquility and wide horizons, perfect for those looking to experience the open countryside and enjoy a sense of solitude away from busier areas. The plateau scenery provides expansive views and a peaceful riding environment.

Q: Are there any specific routes that connect to the Jacobean Route or Via de la Plata?
A: Valverde De La Virgen is directly situated on the historic Jacobean Route, and the region is also part of La Vía de la Plata. Many local touring cycling routes are integrated into these significant historical paths, offering a unique blend of cultural immersion and scenic riding. While specific route names might vary, you'll find numerous options that follow or intersect with these ancient pilgrimage trails.
tips: Carrizo de la Ribera is a small town located on the banks of the Órbigo river. Like many settlements on the Iberian Peninsula, its origin dates back to Celtic times. The most notable monument is the monastery of Santa María de Carrizo from the 12th century.
It is a beautiful and easy section by bike along a dirt track (with beautiful ocher colors) between extensive fields.
From here it is only 300km to Santiago

waypoints.name: Monastery of Santa María de Carrizo
